{"id":"project-LICHEN-gtb6","title":"_check_msb uses Python right-shift which may give unexpected results for negative shift","description":"File: python/src/lichen/schc/codec.py:106-115

_check_msb() computes:
  shift = fd.length_bits - fd.mo_arg
  if (value >> shift) != (fd.target_value >> shift):

If mo_arg > length_bits (invalid configuration), shift is negative. In Python 3, a negative right-shift raises ValueError: 'negative shift count'.

However, this validation happens at compress() time, not at FieldDescriptor creation time. The error message from Python's ValueError is cryptic compared to a clear 'mo_arg exceeds length_bits' message.

Additionally, if mo_arg == length_bits, shift is 0, meaning the entire value must match the target. This is equivalent to MO.EQUAL but using MSB matching. While technically valid, it might indicate a configuration mistake.

Fix: Add explicit validation of mo_arg at FieldDescriptor creation or the start of _check_msb():
  if fd.mo_arg > fd.length_bits:
      raise SchcError(f'{fd.field_id}: mo_arg ({fd.mo_arg}) exceeds length_bits ({fd.length_bits})')

This is related to project-LICHEN-kvwe but affects a different code path.

Category: CORRECTNESS","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:31:10.59073Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:31:10.590772Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-gtb6","title":"_check_msb uses Python right-shift which may give unexpected results for negative shift","description":"File: python/src/lichen/schc/codec.py:106-115

_check_msb() computes:
  shift = fd.length_bits - fd.mo_arg
  if (value >> shift) != (fd.target_value >> shift):

If mo_arg > length_bits (invalid configuration), shift is negative. In Python 3, a negative right-shift raises ValueError: 'negative shift count'.

However, this validation happens at compress() time, not at FieldDescriptor creation time. The error message from Python's ValueError is cryptic compared to a clear 'mo_arg exceeds length_bits' message.

Additionally, if mo_arg == length_bits, shift is 0, meaning the entire value must match the target. This is equivalent to MO.EQUAL but using MSB matching. While technically valid, it might indicate a configuration mistake.

Fix: Add explicit validation of mo_arg at FieldDescriptor creation or the start of _check_msb():
  if fd.mo_arg > fd.length_bits:
      raise SchcError(f'{fd.field_id}: mo_arg ({fd.mo_arg}) exceeds length_bits ({fd.length_bits})')

This is related to project-LICHEN-kvwe but affects a different code path.
